rationale: The last near-miss suggests the baseline training dynamics are already close, so this run avoids disturbing optimization and targets the actual metric directly: the final int8 zlib roundtrip score. I changed the quantizer, not the model. Large 2D float tensors are now quantized after subtracting a per-row mean, and non-2D quantized floats use tensor-centered quantization. The saved means are tiny compared with matrix payloads, but they can recover a meaningful amount of quantization error when rows have DC offsets or asymmetric ranges. This is a new idea category relative to the failed architectural and projection-path experiments, preserves baseline training speed, and specifically optimizes the post-quantization validation number we care about.
title: Centered residual int8 quantization for 2D tensors
